page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8v-4x.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8469C processor running at 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, providing a total of 32 vCPUs. This dedicated allocation ensures no resource sharing at the CPU level, delivering consistent processing performance.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, translating to 12.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it bundles 4.0 GPUs with a combined GPU VRAM of 384 GB. Local storage is not included on this instance, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. However, the high density of GPU VRAM and system memory relative to the 32 dedicated cores makes it highly efficient for memory-intensive parallel computing. This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as deep learning training, large-scale AI inference, data science pipelines, and high-performance computing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8v-4x.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for parallel computing workloads. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8469C x86_64 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs at a 2.6 GHz clock speed. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it includes 4.0 GPUs with a total of 384 GB of VRAM. A key architectural tradeoff is the absence of local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps. This resource-dense configuration is suited for deep learning, AI inference, data science, and high-performance computing workloads.
